title: Margin collapse
description: Adjacent vertical margins merge — a surprise for beginners.

questions.prompt:
The total vertical space between the two

s is:
questions.explanation: Adjacent vertical margins collapse to the LARGER one — 30px, not 60px.

questions.prompt: Which of these DOES NOT collapse margins?
options: A parent and its first child
questions.explanation: Flex (and grid) items don't collapse their margins with each other or the container.

questions.prompt: Fastest fix for a parent-child margin bleed?
options: Give the parent padding (any nonzero amount)
questions.explanation: Any padding, border, or overflow != visible on the parent stops parent-child collapse.
Adjacent margins collapse to the LARGER one — 40px.
Only VERTICAL margins collapse in normal flow.

questions.prompt: What has margin-bottom: auto do on a NORMAL block?
questions.explanation: auto only fills space inside flex/grid — computes to 0 in regular block flow.
Border on the parent stops parent-child margin collapse.
Flex and grid items don't collapse — gap is a cleaner tool there.
